S1.subclaims: (1) `--allow-virtual-attribute-slices` skips ALL target-name validation, including the reserved pseudo-slice names — confirmed at clankerprof/cli.py:361-366 (`attribute.target_slice not in names and not options.allow_virtual_attribute_slices`) and crates/clankerprof-core/src/main.rs:1260-1265. (2) Both renderers unconditionally strip `(gc)`/`(uncollapsible)` rows — confirmed at clankerprof/render.py:922-924 and crates/clankerprof-core/src/slices.rs:315-319. (3) Result is matched time with no owning row, the exact failure the R6-01 spec text says the reserved names exist to prevent — confirmed by reproduction. (4) Control with target `Virtual` proves the sample was attributed then stripped — confirmed.
S1.evidence: Profile: one sample Leaf /app/leaf.py = 10 (built via tests/fixtures/pprof_builder). Slices file sl.yml: slice App paths ['/app/*']. Command (both engines): `uv run clankerprof slices --profile profile.pb --slices sl.yml --attribute 'name:Leaf,to:(gc)' --allow-virtual-attribute-slices` and `cargo run -q -p clankerprof-core --bin clankerprof-rs -- slices ...` (same args). Both exit 0 with exactly {"slices":[],"summary":{"matching_pct":100.0,"matching_time_ns":10,"total_time_ns":10},"tool":"clankerprof_slices"} — 10ns of matched time owned by no row. Control `to:Virtual` (same flag): exit 0, slice Virtual time_ns=10 emitted, proving attribution-then-strip. Without the flag: exit 2 `Unknown slice in --attribute: (gc)`. Spec citation: docs/CLANKERPROF_SPEC.md:619-624 — "The pseudo-output names `(gc)` and `(uncollapsible)` are reserved: a user slice under either name is a validation error in both languages, because such a slice would be attributed and then stripped at render, reporting matched time with no owning row." The R6-01 fix (clankerprof/slices.py:362-366, slices.rs:225-229) only guards configured slice names; the virtual-attribute opt-in reintroduces the identical failure through the attribute target. Identical-but-harmful in both languages; violates the spec's stated invariant.
S1.fix_sketch: Validate attribute targets against the reserved names unconditionally (the opt-in only waives the must-name-a-configured-slice rule, never the reservation). Python clankerprof/cli.py, in the attribute loop (~line 358): before the `not in names` check add `if attribute.target_slice in {GC_PSEUDO_SLICE, UNCOLLAPSIBLE_PSEUDO_SLICE}: raise ValueError(f"Attribute target names reserved pseudo-slice name: {attribute.target_slice}. The names (gc) and (uncollapsible) are reserved for analyzer pseudo-outputs.")` (import the constants from clankerprof.slices). Rust crates/clankerprof-core/src/main.rs (~line 1260): mirror byte-identically before the `!names.contains(...) && !allow_virtual_attribute_slices` check using slices::GC_PSEUDO_SLICE/UNCOLLAPSIBLE_PSEUDO_SLICE. Extend the spec paragraph at docs/CLANKERPROF_SPEC.md:619 to say the reservation also applies to `--attribute` targets, including virtual ones under `--allow-virtual-attribute-slices`; add a parity test asserting the identical exit-2 envelope in both languages and update docs/CLANKERPROF_PARITY.md's reserved-name row.

S2.subclaims: (1) Neither importer bounds `profile.primary_value_index` against the declared value types (or sample value arity): Python clankerprof/facts.py:374-379 rejects only non-integers/> i64::MAX/negative; Rust facts.rs:251-265 identical — confirmed. (2) Out-of-range index silently falls back to `values[0]` per sample: clankerprof/model.py:60-66 and crates/clankerprof-core/src/model.rs:92-99 — confirmed (falls back to values[0]=1, not literally 'value zero' as the claim's title says, but the substantive claim — wrong metric selected silently — holds and matches the auditor's own repro output of 1). (3) Summary validation is computed from the same fallback values, so an internally consistent malformed artifact self-validates — confirmed (facts.py:325-331). (4) Spec violation: partially — spec line 142's '(with the fallbacks from primary-value selection)' textually covers the values[0] arm (SPEC.md:59-60), so the runtime behavior is not a literal contradiction of SPEC.md:141-145; but nothing documents accepting an index no selection rule can produce, and the requirement's own rationale (SPEC.md:133-137: index made mandatory because 'silently defaulting it to 0 contradicted the documented last-declared-type selection fallback') plus the existing negative-index rejection show the strict-import intent this hole defeats.
S2.evidence: Built a profile with sample_types [samples/count, cpu/nanoseconds], default_sample_type 'cpu', one sample values (1,100); `uv run clankerprof facts --profile profile.pb --output facts.json` exports primary_value_index=1, total_primary_value=100. Mutated only profile.primary_value_index -> 99 and summary.total_primary_value -> 1 (consistent with the values[0] fallback). Commands: `uv run clankerprof slices --facts facts_bad.json --slices sl.yml` and `cargo run -q -p clankerprof-core --bin clankerprof-rs -- slices --facts facts_bad.json --slices sl.yml`. Both exit 0 and byte-identically report slice App time_ns=1, summary {matching_time_ns:1, total_time_ns:1} — the 'samples' count metric (via values[0]) instead of the declared-primary cpu metric (100), with no error or fallback record. Spec citations: SPEC.md:52-57 — selection rules can only yield a declared type's position or 0, so 99 is unreachable from any documented selection and is malformed metadata; SPEC.md:133-137 — index required precisely because silent index defaulting mis-selects the metric; SPEC.md:143-145 — v2 importers 'must validate frame and string indexes ... and reject summaries that disagree with the reconstructed samples' (value-index range is the one index left unvalidated); negative index already rejected (facts.py:378-379, facts.rs:264-265), showing the acceptance of 99 is an oversight, not a documented decision. Genuine correctness defect: malformed facts silently select and report the wrong metric with a self-consistent summary, identical-but-harmful in both languages.
S2.fix_sketch: Reject an out-of-range primary_value_index at v2 profile-meta validation in both importers, byte-identical message. Python clankerprof/facts.py `_validated_profile_meta` (after line 379, once value_types is known): `if value_types: ok = primary_value_index < len(value_types)` else `ok = primary_value_index == 0`; on failure raise `ValueError(f"Sample facts primary_value_index {primary_value_index} is out of range for {len(value_types)} declared value types.")`. Rust crates/clankerprof-core/src/facts.rs (~line 265, after value_types parsing): identical check/message. Exporters always satisfy this (decode-time selection yields a declared position or 0), so no artifact churn. Keep the per-sample values[0] fallback (SPEC.md:59-60) for ragged samples under a valid index. Update SPEC.md:143 to add the primary value index to the importer must-validate list, add a parity test asserting the identical exit-2 envelope in both CLIs, and update docs/CLANKERPROF_PARITY.md.

S3.subclaims: (1) Mixed named-capture + numeric backreference `(?P<x>/app)\1` is valid Python but rejected at compile by fancy-regex — confirmed: Rust exits 2, Python exits 0/Hit. (2) Conditional pattern `(?P<x>/app)/a(?(x)|x)` compiles in both but matches only in Python — confirmed: Python Hit/7, Rust Other/7, both exit 0 (silent semantic divergence, fail-open direction). (3) These are outside the documented exception list — confirmed: SPEC.md:507-520 pins exactly three matching-semantics edges ($, (?i) special case folds, \w/\s/\b Unicode membership) and R5-02's document-and-pin resolution (goalloop.audit.md:63) covers only those; SPEC.md:505-507 affirmatively claims the dialect includes backreferences and that fancy-regex 'accepts that dialect'. (4) Full engine parity is not achievable without replacing/wrapping the engine — honest assessment: fancy-regex rejects numbered-backref-with-named-groups by design and diverges on conditional-group semantics; no engine knobs exist (same conclusion R5-02 reached for its edges).
S3.evidence: Case 1: profile sample (Leaf /app/app under T) = 7; config {"T": {"Hit": "regex:(?P<x>/app)\\1"}}. `uv run clankerprof targets --profile profile1.pb --config cfg1.json` -> exit 0, category Hit time_ns 7. `cargo run -q -p clankerprof-core --bin clankerprof-rs -- targets --profile profile1.pb --config cfg1.json` -> exit 2, {"error": "Invalid regex pattern '(?P<x>/app)\\1': Error compiling regex: Numbered backref/call not allowed because named group was used, use a named backref instead", "ok": false}. Case 2: leaf path /app/a, pattern regex:(?P<x>/app)/a(?(x)|x): Python exit 0 category Hit/7; Rust exit 0 category Other/7. Spec citations: SPEC.md:504-507 — 'follow Python's regular-expression dialect, including lookaround and backreferences; the Rust implementation compiles them with an engine (fancy-regex) that accepts that dialect' (falsified by case 1); SPEC.md:509-511 — divergences limited to 'these known edges' ($ anchor, İ case-fold, \w/\s/\b), conditionals and mixed backrefs absent; PARITY.md:153 claims Python-dialect regexes 'load and match in both languages within the pinned semantic subset'. Case 1 violates the compile-acceptance claim (though it fails closed, exit 2, the safe direction); case 2 is a silent categorization divergence not covered by any pinned edge — a genuine parity defect against the published contract.
S3.fix_sketch: Parity is not fully achievable on fancy-regex; adopt a split resolution mirroring R5-02. (a) Case 1 (mechanical, restores parity): in Rust regex compilation (crates/clankerprof-core/src/targets.rs compile path ~442), pre-rewrite numeric backreferences `\N` to `\k<name>` when group N is a named group (group names/positions are recoverable by a small scanner over the pattern's capture-group opens, skipping escapes and char classes); alternatively, if rewriting is judged too fragile, document the rejection as an engine-native compile edge — it already fails closed with the shared 'Invalid regex pattern' envelope prefix. (b) Case 2 (impossible to unify): document-and-pin — extend the SPEC.md:507-520 edge list with conditional-group `(?(group)yes|no)` semantics as engine-native and outside the parity guarantee, and extend `test_clankerprof_regex_engine_edge_semantics_are_pinned` (tests/test_clankerprof_rust_parity.py:4233) to pin both new behaviors against drift. (c) Soften the overclaims: SPEC.md:505-507 'accepts that dialect' -> 'accepts the overwhelming majority of that dialect; patterns fancy-regex cannot compile fail closed with exit 2', and update PARITY.md:153 accordingly.

S4.subclaims: (1) Zero signed matching total renders every slice pct as 0 — confirmed (unfiltered run: A time_ns=10 pct=0, B time_ns=-10 pct=0, matching_time_ns=0), matching SPEC.md:611-613 and the R6-02 fix. (2) The `%`-threshold filter has an extra nonzero-total conjunct that drops every row at zero total regardless of threshold — confirmed at clankerprof/render.py:934-938 (`if total and _f64_ratio(...) >= threshold`) and crates/clankerprof-core/src/slices.rs:323-325 (`total != 0 && ... >= threshold`). (3) Thresholds 0% and -1% are satisfied by every rendered-0% row yet remove all rows — confirmed in both engines. (4) Not documented as deliberate — confirmed: SPEC.md:757-761 only covers by-slice value parsing; SPEC.md:190 says '--by-slice threshold selection' shares the percentage computation contract; SPEC.md:597-599 says negative aggregates 'are signed data and must be reported'; goalloop.audit.md R6-02 shows the zero arm was the intended contract and the guard is a leftover inconsistency.
S4.evidence: Profile: FuncA /a/x.py = +10, FuncB /b/x.py = -10; slices A paths /a/*, B paths /b/*. Baseline both engines (`uv run clankerprof slices --profile profile.pb --slices sl.yml` / `cargo run -q -p clankerprof-core --bin clankerprof-rs -- slices ...`): exit 0, [('A',10,0),('B',-10,0)], summary {matching_pct:0, matching_time_ns:0, total_time_ns:0}. With `--by-slice=0%`: both exit 0 with {"slices":[],"summary":{"matching_pct":0,"matching_time_ns":0,"total_time_ns":0}}. With `--by-slice=-1%`: both exit 0, slices []. Each row's rendered percentage is 0 and 0 >= 0 / 0 >= -1 hold, so the rows satisfy the threshold; they are removed only by the total-nonzero guard. Spec citations: SPEC.md:611-613 — 'A zero matching total ... renders every dependent percentage as 0 — never a silent fallback'; SPEC.md:188-191 — 'percentage bytes and --by-slice threshold selection stay identical' under the same percentage contract, i.e. threshold selection is defined over those rendered percentages; SPEC.md:597-599 — negative pseudo-output aggregates are signed data that 'must be reported' (rationale extends to signed slice rows). Identical-but-harmful in both languages: nonzero signed rows vanish under thresholds they satisfy; the guard contradicts the documented zero-arm percentage contract that R6-02 (goalloop.audit.md:78) established.
S4.fix_sketch: Apply the zero-arm rule inside the threshold predicate instead of short-circuiting. Python clankerprof/render.py:934-938: `selected_slices = [item for item in selected_slices if (_f64_ratio(item.time_ns, total) * 100 if total else 0.0) >= threshold]`. Rust crates/clankerprof-core/src/slices.rs:323-325: `selected_slices.retain(|slice| { let pct = if total != 0 { slice.time_ns as f64 / total as f64 * 100.0 } else { 0.0 }; pct >= threshold });`. This keeps all rows for thresholds <= 0 at zero total and still drops everything for positive thresholds (0 >= t false), byte-identically in both languages. Add a sentence to SPEC.md near 611-613 ('at a zero matching total, %-threshold selection compares against the rendered 0 percentages'), extend test_clankerprof_zero_sum_filtered_slices_use_zero_percentages (tests/test_clankerprof.py:6861) with --by-slice=0%/-1% assertions, and mirror in the Rust parity matrix row at docs/CLANKERPROF_PARITY.md:147.

S5.evidence: Fixture: PprofFixtureBuilder profile with stacks [PosLeaf,T]=+10 and [NegLeaf,T]=-10, targets config {"T":{"Pos":"pos.py","Neg":"neg.py"}} (scratch .goalloop-support/triage7/S5/). `uv run clankerprof targets --profile profile.pb --config targets.json --format json` -> rc=0 with Pos{time_ns:10,pct:0}, Neg{time_ns:-10,pct:0} (projection accounting is correct). `--format simple-csv` -> rc=0 printing ONLY the header line `Parent Function,Category,CPU %,Top 3 Callsites,Top Leaf Functions`; `cargo run -q -p clankerprof-core --bin clankerprof-rs -- targets ... --format simple-csv` -> rc=0, identical header-only output. Drop site verified: clankerprof/render.py:575-576 `if simplified and abs(pct) < 0.1 and category != "Other": continue`; crates/clankerprof-core/src/render.rs:324-326 identical. Spec docs/CLANKERPROF_SPEC.md:339-340 requires complete target accounting, and :348-350 states the rationale 'rows with negative shares of any magnitude must be rendered — sample values are signed, and dropping them breaks additivity'; :344-346 (R4-03 fix) defines zero-total pct as 0, so abs(0)<0.1 defeats the R4-06 negative-row protection exactly at zero totals. Test tests/test_clankerprof.py:6421-6428 confirmed to assert only absence of inf/NaN for simple-csv. Precedent: HEAD's own R6-03 fix (commit 755ccfc) confirmed the identical zero-cancelled-parent wholesale drop as a defect for slice rollups ('erasing nonzero negative rows against the additivity rule'), so this is not a documented deliberate asymmetry — goalloop.audit.md contains no refutation covering it. Bonus repro strengthening the defect: with Pos=+100000/Neg=-10 (pct approx -0.01%), Python simple-csv rc=0 emitted only the Pos row, silently dropping the negative row — directly violating the literal spec sentence 'rows with negative shares of any magnitude must be rendered' even for nonzero totals.
S5.subclaims: (1) Zero-total signed target renders header-only simple-csv in both languages: reproduced. (2) JSON renderers keep both signed rows with pct 0: reproduced. (3) Covering test only checks inf/NaN: verified at tests/test_clankerprof.py:6421-6428. (4) Violates documented contract: verified via SPEC 339-350 additivity/negative-row rationale plus the R6-03 precedent treating the same drop pattern as a defect; additionally found the gate drops tiny-magnitude negative shares (|pct|<0.1) at nonzero totals, a literal violation of SPEC:348-350 that the same fix resolves. Auditor's 'vacuous requirement' framing is accurate: the zero-total pct:=0 definition (R4-03) interacts with the abs() gate (R4-06) to erase all evidence of nonzero signed costs.
S5.fix_sketch: Make the simplified noise gate never omit rows with negative signed time and never omit nonzero rows under a zero total. Python clankerprof/render.py:575-576: replace with `if (simplified and category != "Other" and stats.cpu_time >= 0 and abs(pct) < 0.1 and (total != 0 or stats.cpu_time == 0)): continue` (i.e., omit only nonnegative-time rows whose share is small, and under total==0 only exactly-zero rows). Rust crates/clankerprof-core/src/render.rs:324-326 mirror: `if simplified && category != "Other" && stats.cpu_time >= 0 && pct.abs() < 0.1 && (total != 0 || stats.cpu_time == 0) { continue; }`. Apply to both simple-csv and the compat simplified CSV path if it shares the gate. Tighten SPEC:346-350 to state: under a zero parent total only exactly-zero rows may be omitted, and rows with negative time_ns are never omitted regardless of rendered share. Extend test_clankerprof_zero_total_targets_render_without_division_errors (or add a new @covers test) to assert Pos/Neg rows appear in simple-csv for the zero-total and tiny-negative-share cases; add a parity-matrix case and update the docs/CLANKERPROF_PARITY.md:221 row wording.

S6.evidence: Fixture: profile with one stack [Block(/app/block.py), S(/app/s.py)] value 10; TOML scope config `[cost_kind] "AppWork" = "path:app/**"` + `[[scope]] label="Audited" function="S" exclude_descendants="name_eq:Block"` and a copy with only `exclude_descendants` -> `exclude_descendents` (scratch .goalloop-support/triage7/S6/). `uv run clankerprof scopes --profile profile.pb --config good.toml` -> rc=0, boundary Audited samples:0 total_time_ns:0 (exclusion works). Same command with typo.toml -> rc=0, no warning, Audited samples:1 total_time_ns:10 with bucket Other / category AppWork time_ns:10 — exclusion silently disabled. `cargo run -q -p clankerprof-core --bin clankerprof-rs -- scopes ...`: good -> samples 0 total 0 rc=0; typo -> samples 1 total 10 rc=0. Code verified: clankerprof/cli.py:923-980 `_load_boundaries` reads known keys via raw_boundary.get(...) with no unknown-key allowlist; Rust scopes.rs does the same. Contract context: docs/CLANKERPROF_SPEC.md:281-284 establishes the repo's own principle for rule packs — 'unknown top-level keys and unknown match-rule entry keys are validation errors (typos never silently disable a rule)' — and the scopes section (SPEC ~397-420) validates every other scope-entry shape strictly (count vocabulary, label/name string-ness, empty expression tables, top-level slices type) but nowhere documents unknown scope keys as deliberately ignored. goalloop.audit.md has no refutation covering this.
S6.subclaims: (1) Typoed exclude_descendants key silently accepted, exclusion disabled, both engines: reproduced (0/0 -> 1/10). (2) No warning emitted: verified (clean JSON rc=0). (3) No allowlist in either loader: verified in cli.py _load_boundaries and scopes.rs. (4) Not documented-deliberate: verified — spec is silent on unknown scope-entry keys while explicitly declaring the anti-typo principle for rule packs and strict validation for every other scope field; behavior is identical across engines (no parity break), so this is a genuine correctness defect (silent semantic misconfiguration), not a parity defect.
S6.fix_sketch: Add an unknown-key allowlist for scope/boundary entries in both loaders with a shared byte-identical message. Python clankerprof/cli.py _load_boundaries: after `raw_boundary = cast(...)`, check `unknown = set(raw_boundary) - {"label","name","function","selector","patterns","predicates","count","rollup","bucket","attributables","exclude_descendants"}` (use the actual alias set consumed by _boundary_predicate_value/_aliased_mapping_value) and raise `ValueError(f"Unknown {section_name} field: {sorted(unknown)[0]}.")` (keys must already be strings per the YAML string-key rule; sort for determinism). Rust crates/clankerprof-core/src/scopes.rs boundary-entry parsing: same allowlist and identical message. Consider the same treatment for [scope.rollup]/owner sub-tables only if their key spaces are fixed-shape (rollup values are free-form names, so restrict to the entry level). Add a spec sentence in the scopes section mirroring SPEC:281-284, a Python test (typo key -> exit 2, shared message) under @covers, a Rust unit test, a parity-matrix case, and a docs/CLANKERPROF_PARITY.md row.

S7.evidence: Fixture: profile with one sample Leaf(/else/leaf.py)=10; slices.yaml with App paths ["/app/**"] and Default default:true (scratch .goalloop-support/triage7/S7/). Baseline `uv run clankerprof slices --profile profile.pb --slices slices.yaml` -> [('Default',10)]. With `--attribute 'name:,to:App'` -> rc=0, [('App',10)] — the empty-value predicate matched every frame and reassigned all ownership. Control: `--filter 'name:'` -> rc=2 {"error": "Filter must be '<key>:<value>': name:"} — regular filters DO reject empty values (clankerprof/cli.py:345-346 `if not key or not value`). Rust: `cargo run -q ... slices ... --attribute 'name:,to:App'` -> rc=0 [('App',10)]; `--filter 'name:'` -> rc=2 same envelope. Code verified: Python _parse_attribute (cli.py:465-483) checks only `if not separator` after partition(":") — empty value accepted; attribute validation at cli.py:356-368 checks only key membership; slices.py:121-122 `return value in frame.name` makes '' match everything. Rust main.rs/slices.rs mirror this. docs/CLANKERPROF_PARITY.md:132 claims 'Attribution overrides, including invalid/duplicate filter rejection — covered', and no spec text documents empty attribute values as a deliberate match-all idiom; the established grammar for the shared filter-key vocabulary ('<key>:<value>', both parts required) is enforced everywhere except this path.
S7.subclaims: (1) Empty-value attribute accepted and matches all frames, reassigning ownership, both engines: reproduced ([('Default',10)] -> [('App',10)]). (2) Regular filters reject the same shape: verified rc=2 both engines. (3) Acceptance sites as cited: verified in _parse_attribute / attribute validation (Python) and main.rs/slices.rs (Rust). (4) Contradicts documented invalid-filter coverage claim (PARITY.md:132) and the filter grammar; not documented as deliberate anywhere; behavior is identical cross-engine (correctness footgun, not parity break). Severity 'minor' is fair — it requires an explicit malformed flag, but a trailing-colon typo silently corrupts all attribution, consistent with the repo's fail-closed principle.
S7.fix_sketch: Reject empty key or value in attribute rules with the existing message. Python clankerprof/cli.py _parse_attribute: change `if not separator:` to `if not separator or not key or not value:` after the key/value partition (raising `Attribute rule filter must be '<key>:<value>': {raw}`) — or equivalently add `if not attribute.key or not attribute.value` next to the key-vocabulary check at cli.py:356-360 for a single validation site. Rust main.rs attribute parsing (~1175-1188): same guard with the byte-identical message. Add a Python test asserting exit 2 + envelope for `--attribute 'name:,to:App'` and `--attribute ':x,to:App'`, a parity-matrix error case, and correct/extend the docs/CLANKERPROF_PARITY.md:132 row to name empty-predicate rejection.

S8.evidence: Fixture: nonempty pprof with one sample Leaf(/app/leaf.py)=7, written as gzip.compress(raw) + b'\x00'*16 (scratch .goalloop-support/triage7/S8/padded.pb.gz). `uv run clankerprof facts --profile padded.pb.gz` -> rc=0 with the full v2 facts payload (summary total_primary_value:7, sample_count:1). `cargo run -q -p clankerprof-core --bin clankerprof-rs -- facts --profile padded.pb.gz` -> rc=2 {"error": "invalid gzip header", "ok": false}. Root cause verified: Python proto.py:246-253 calls gzip.decompress; CPython's gzip._GzipReader._read_eof deliberately consumes trailing/inter-member zero padding ('Gzip files can be padded with zeroes and still have archives ... See http://www.gzip.org/#faq8' — confirmed by inspecting the installed 3.11 source and by direct experiment: zeros -> OK, nonzero garbage -> BadGzipFile). Rust proto.rs:294-304 uses flate2 MultiGzDecoder behind a comment claiming 'MultiGzDecoder matches Python's gzip.decompress' — false for zero padding: MultiGzDecoder tries to parse the padding as a next member header and errors. Contract: docs/CLANKERPROF_SPEC.md:20-23 requires decoding gzip streams with multiple members ('silently truncating ... is a defect'), and SPEC:813-816 claims the facts command emits the same v2 payload byte-for-byte as Python for gzipped profiles; PARITY.md:34 and :155 claim gzip decoding parity coverage. Neither spec nor goalloop.audit.md documents zero-padding as a deliberate asymmetry; R1-10 (goalloop.audit.md:13) and R6 cluster T establish input-domain gzip parity as a binding goal. Zero padding after a gzip member is conventional (gzip FAQ 8; real gzip(1) ignores trailing zeros), so this is a genuine accepted-input parity defect, achievable with a Rust-only fix.
S8.subclaims: (1) Python accepts zero-padded gzip profile with correct facts: reproduced rc=0. (2) Rust rejects identical bytes rc=2 'invalid gzip header': reproduced. (3) proto.rs comment claims false equivalence: verified at proto.rs:294-297. (4) Python semantics characterized precisely: CPython skips any run of 0x00 after each member (also between members); nonzero trailing garbage raises BadGzipFile (which decode_profile_bytes' `except OSError` then routes to a raw-protobuf parse attempt) — the fix must replicate only the zero-skip to close this finding. Parity is achievable (no engine-dialect limitation), so document-and-pin is not warranted.
S8.fix_sketch: Rust-only decoder fix in crates/clankerprof-core/src/proto.rs maybe_gzip_decompress: replace the single MultiGzDecoder pass with a per-member loop that mirrors CPython: `let mut rest: &[u8] = data; let mut payload = Vec::new(); loop { let mut dec = flate2::bufread::GzDecoder::new(rest); dec.read_to_end(&mut payload)?; rest = dec.into_inner(); while let Some((0, tail)) = rest.split_first() { rest = tail; } // gzip FAQ #8 zero padding, matches CPython _read_eof; if rest.is_empty() { break; } if !rest.starts_with(&[0x1f, 0x8b]) { return Err(..."invalid gzip header"-equivalent); } }` (bufread::GzDecoder consumes exactly one member from the BufRead slice, so into_inner resumes at the trailer end; this also preserves R1-10 multi-member behavior and tolerates zero padding between members exactly as CPython does). Fix the false comment. Add a Rust unit test (zero-padded single member, zero padding between members) and a Python+parity case (facts-zero-padded-gzip) pinning byte-identical facts output; add a spec sentence under the decoding contract ('zero padding after any member is consumed, per gzip FAQ 8, matching CPython gzip') and a docs/CLANKERPROF_PARITY.md row.
